The five varieties of houses of royal astrologers, preceptors and
physicians should measure 40, 36, 32, 28 and 24 cubits in breadth,
and their length should exceed the breadth by a sixth.
[Their length would be in order 46J, 42, 37|, 32^ and 28
cubits,]
Next he speaks of the height and number of rooms.
It is auspicious for houses to have the same height as their
breadth. The length of houses with single halls should be double
their breadth.
